function.name: mcp_brave_search_brave_local_search
function.description:
Brave Local Search API provides enrichments for location search results. Access to this API is available only through the Brave Search API Pro plans; confirm the user's plan before using this tool (if the user does not have a Pro plan, use the brave_web_search tool). Searches for local businesses and places using Brave's Local Search API. Best for queries related to physical locations, businesses, restaurants, services, etc.
    
    Returns detailed information including:
        - Business names and addresses
        - Ratings and review counts
        - Phone numbers and opening hours

    Use this when the query implies 'near me', 'in my area', or mentions specific locations (e.g., 'in San Francisco'). This tool automatically falls back to brave_web_search if no local results are found.

country.description: Search query country, where the results come from. The country string is limited to 2 character country codes of supported countries.

search_lang.description: Search language preference. The 2 or more character language code for which the search results are provided.

ui_lang.description: The language of the UI. The 2 or more character language code for which the search results are provided.

count.description: Number of results (1-20, default 10). Applies only to web search results (i.e., has no effect on locations, news, videos, etc.)

safesearch.description: Filters search results for adult content. The following values are supported: 'off' - No filtering. 'moderate' - Filters explicit content (e.g., images and videos), but allows adult domains in search results. 'strict' - Drops all adult content from search results. The default value is 'moderate'.

freshness.description: Filters search results by when they were discovered. The following values are supported: 'pd' - Discovered within the last 24 hours. 'pw' - Discovered within the last 7 days. 'pm' - Discovered within the last 31 days. 'py' - Discovered within the last 365 days. 'YYYY-MM-DDtoYYYY-MM-DD' - Timeframe is also supported by specifying the date range e.g. [REDACTED_PHONE]to[REDACTED_PHONE].

text_decorations.description: Whether display strings (e.g. result snippets) should include decoration markers (e.g. highlighting characters).

function.name: mcp_brave_search_brave_news_search
function.description:
This tool searches for news articles using Brave's News Search API based on the user's query. Use it when you need current news information, breaking news updates, or articles about specific topics, events, or entities.
    
    When to use:
        - Finding recent news articles on specific topics
        - Getting breaking news updates
        - Researching current events or trending stories
        - Gathering news sources and headlines for analysis

    Returns a JSON list of news-related results with title, url, and description. Some results may contain snippets of text from the article.
    
    When relaying results in markdown-supporting environments, always cite sources with hyperlinks.
    
    Examples:
        - "According to [Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/technology/china-bans/), China bans uncertified and recalled power banks on planes".
        - "The [New York Times](https://www.nytimes.com/2025/06/27/us/technology/ev-sales.html) reports that Tesla's EV sales have increased by 20%".
        - "According to [BBC News](https://www.bbc.com/news/world-europe-65910000), the UK government has announced a new policy to support renewable energy".

function.name: mcp_brave_search_brave_summarizer
function.description:
Retrieves AI-generated summaries of web search results using Brave's Summarizer API. This tool processes search results to create concise, coherent summaries of information gathered from multiple sources.

    When to use:

    - When you need a concise overview of complex topics from multiple sources
    - For quick fact-checking or getting key points without reading full articles
    - When providing users with summarized information that synthesizes various perspectives
    - For research tasks requiring distilled information from web searches

    Returns a text summary that consolidates information from the search results. Optional features include inline references to source URLs and additional entity information.

    Requirements: Must first perform a web search using brave_web_search with summary=true parameter. Requires a Pro AI subscription to access the summarizer functionality.

key.description: The key is equal to value of field key as part of the Summarizer response model.

entity_info.description: Returns extra entities info with the summary response.

inline_references.description: Adds inline references to the summary response.

function.name: mcp_context7_query_docs
function.description:
Retrieves and queries up-to-date documentation and code examples from Context7 for any programming library or framework.

You must call 'Resolve Context7 Library ID' tool first to obtain the exact Context7-compatible library ID required to use this tool, UNLESS the user explicitly provides a library ID in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version' in their query.

Do not call this tool more than 3 times per question.

libraryId.description: Exact Context7-compatible library ID (e.g., '/mongodb/docs', '/vercel/next.js', '/supabase/supabase', '/vercel/next.js/v14.3.0-canary.87') retrieved from 'resolve-library-id' or directly from user query in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version'.

query.description: The question or task you need help with. Be specific and include relevant details. Good: 'How to set up authentication with JWT in Express.js' or 'React useEffect cleanup function examples'. Bad: 'auth' or 'hooks'. The query is sent to the Context7 API for processing. Do not include any sensitive or confidential information such as API keys, passwords, credentials, personal data, or proprietary code in your query.

function.name: mcp_context7_resolve_library_id
function.description:
Resolves a package/product name to a Context7-compatible library ID and returns matching libraries.

You MUST call this function before 'Query Documentation' tool to obtain a valid Context7-compatible library ID UNLESS the user explicitly provides a library ID in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version' in their query.

Each result includes:
- Library ID: Context7-compatible identifier (format: /org/project)
- Name: Library or package name
- Description: Short summary
- Code Snippets: Number of available code examples
- Source Reputation: Authority indicator (High, Medium, Low, or Unknown)
- Benchmark Score: Quality indicator (100 is the highest score)
- Versions: List of versions if available. Use one of those versions if the user provides a version in their query. The format of the version is /org/project/version.

For best results, select libraries based on name match, source reputation, snippet coverage, benchmark score, and relevance to your use case.

Selection Process:
1. Analyze the query to understand what library/package the user is looking for
2. Return the most relevant match based on:
- Name similarity to the query (exact matches prioritized)
- Description relevance to the query's intent
- Documentation coverage (prioritize libraries with higher Code Snippet counts)
- Source reputation (consider libraries with High or Medium reputation more authoritative)
- Benchmark Score: Quality indicator (100 is the highest score)

Response Format:
- Return the selected library ID in a clearly marked section
- Provide a brief explanation for why this library was chosen
- If multiple good matches exist, acknowledge this but proceed with the most relevant one
- If no good matches exist, clearly state this and suggest query refinements

For ambiguous queries, request clarification before proceeding with a best-guess match.

IMPORTANT: Do not call this tool more than 3 times per question. If you cannot find what you need after 3 calls, use the best result you have.

query.description: The question or task you need help with. This is used to rank library results by relevance to what the user is trying to accomplish. The query is sent to the Context7 API for processing. Do not include any sensitive or confidential information such as API keys, passwords, credentials, personal data, or proprietary code in your query.

libraryName.description: Library name to search for and retrieve a Context7-compatible library ID. Use the official library name with proper punctuation — e.g., 'Next.js' instead of 'nextjs', 'Customer.io' instead of 'customerio', 'Three.js' instead of 'threejs'.

      Best for: Finding current information, news, facts, people, companies, or answering questions about any topic.

function.name: [REDACTED_TOKEN]
function.description:
A detailed tool for dynamic and reflective problem-solving through thoughts.
This tool helps analyze problems through a flexible thinking process that can adapt and evolve.
Each thought can build on, question, or revise previous insights as understanding deepens.

When to use this tool:
- Breaking down complex problems into steps
- Planning and design with room for revision
- Analysis that might need course correction
- Problems where the full scope might not be clear initially
- Problems that require a multi-step solution
- Tasks that need to maintain context over multiple steps
- Situations where irrelevant information needs to be filtered out

Key features:
- You can adjust total_thoughts up or down as you progress
- You can question or revise previous thoughts
- You can add more thoughts even after reaching what seemed like the end
- You can express uncertainty and explore alternative approaches
- Not every thought needs to build linearly - you can branch or backtrack
- Generates a solution hypothesis
- Verifies the hypothesis based on the Chain of Thought steps
- Repeats the process until satisfied
- Provides a correct answer

Parameters explained:
- thought: Your current thinking step, which can include:
  * Regular analytical steps
  * Revisions of previous thoughts
  * Questions about previous decisions
  * Realizations about needing more analysis
  * Changes in approach
  * Hypothesis generation
  * Hypothesis verification
- nextThoughtNeeded: True if you need more thinking, even if at what seemed like the end
- thoughtNumber: Current number in sequence (can go beyond initial total if needed)
- totalThoughts: Current estimate of thoughts needed (can be adjusted up/down)
- isRevision: A boolean indicating if this thought revises previous thinking
- revisesThought: If is_revision is true, which thought number is being reconsidered
- branchFromThought: If branching, which thought number is the branching point
- branchId: Identifier for the current branch (if any)
- needsMoreThoughts: If reaching end but realizing more thoughts needed

You should:
1. Start with an initial estimate of needed thoughts, but be ready to adjust
2. Feel free to question or revise previous thoughts
3. Don't hesitate to add more thoughts if needed, even at the "end"
4. Express uncertainty when present
5. Mark thoughts that revise previous thinking or branch into new paths
6. Ignore information that is irrelevant to the current step
7. Generate a solution hypothesis when appropriate
8. Verify the hypothesis based on the Chain of Thought steps
9. Repeat the process until satisfied with the solution
10. Provide a single, ideally correct answer as the final output
11. Only set nextThoughtNeeded to false when truly done and a satisfactory answer is reached

nextThoughtNeeded.description: Whether another thought step is needed

totalThoughts.description: Estimated total thoughts needed (numeric value, e.g., 5, 10)

function.description:
Targeted find-and-replace edits in files. Use this instead of sed/awk in terminal. Uses fuzzy matching (9 strategies) so minor whitespace/indentation differences won't break it. Returns a unified diff. Auto-runs syntax checks after editing.

Replace mode (default): find a unique string and replace it.
Patch mode: apply V4A multi-file patches for bulk changes.

mode.description: Edit mode: 'replace' for targeted find-and-replace, 'patch' for V4A multi-file patches

path.description: File path to edit (required for 'replace' mode)

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'replace' mode). Must be unique in the file unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'replace' mode). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: Replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false)

patch.description:
V4A format patch content (required for 'patch' mode). Format:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: path/to/file
@@ context hint @@
 context line
-removed line
+added line
*** End Patch

function.description: Read a text file with line numbers and pagination. Use this instead of cat/head/tail in terminal. Output format: 'LINE_NUM|CONTENT'. Suggests similar filenames if not found. Use offset and limit for large files. Reads exceeding ~100K characters are rejected; use offset and limit to read specific sections of large files. NOTE: Cannot read images or binary files — use vision_analyze for images.

path.description: Path to the file to read (absolute, relative, or ~/path)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed, default: 1)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read (default: 500, max: 2000)

function.name: search_files

Search file contents or find files by name. Use this instead of grep/rg/find/ls in terminal. Ripgrep-backed, faster than shell equivalents.

Content search (target='content'): Regex search inside files. Output modes: full matches with line numbers, file paths only, or match counts.

File search (target='files'): Find files by glob pattern (e.g., '*.py', '*config*'). Also use this instead of ls — results sorted by modification time.

pattern.description: Regex pattern for content search, or glob pattern (e.g., '*.py') for file search

target.description: 'content' searches inside file contents, 'files' searches for files by name

path.description: Directory or file to search in (default: current working directory)

file_glob.description: Filter files by pattern in grep mode (e.g., '*.py' to only search Python files)

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 50)

offset.description: Skip first N results for pagination (default: 0)

output_mode.description: Output format for grep mode: 'content' shows matching lines with line numbers, 'files_only' lists file paths, 'count' shows match counts per file

context.description: Number of context lines before and after each match (grep mode only)

path.description: Path to the file to write (will be created if it doesn't exist, overwritten if it does)

# Nexa Paraguay — AI Agent Guide

**Live:** https://nexa.paragu-ai.com  
**Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/nexa-paraguay  
**Docker service:** nexa_web (1 replica)

## Core Architecture

- **Framework:** `@ai-whisperers/*` package ecosystem (client imports via npm `file:` links)
- **Pages Router** (Next.js 16, React 19, TypeScript 5)
- **Content:** JSON-driven — `content/es.json`, `nexa-pages/*.json`, `images.json`, `site.json`
- **Loading:** `src/lib/loader.ts` with 60s in-memory TTL cache (replaces raw `readFileSync`)
- **Section map:** 26 components in `SECTION_MAP` (see `docs/02-site/COMPONENT_REGISTRY.md`)
- **Types:** 30+ interfaces in `src/types.ts` define the full data contract
- **Styling:** `src/theme.ts` — 20+ color tokens, all inline styles reference this

## Key Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `content/es.json` | All text content (100KB, 15+ page sections) |
| `content/en.json`, `nl.json`, `de.json` | Multi-locale content (currently only ES served) |
| `nexa-pages/*.json` | Page configs — section ordering + content key references |
| `images.json` | Image manifest — 82 entries across 14 categories |
| `site.json` | Domain, features, booking URL, social links |
| `src/pages/[slug].tsx` | Main page renderer — SSR, SECTION_MAP dispatch |
| `src/pages/index.tsx` | Homepage renderer (separate file) |
| `src/pages/blog/[slug].tsx` | Individual blog post renderer — MDX-driven SSR |
| `src/lib/loader.ts` | Shared JSON loader with 60s cache |
| `src/types.ts` | 30+ TypeScript interfaces |
| `src/theme.ts` | Brand design tokens |
| `scripts/screenshot-all.mjs` | Full-page screenshot automation — builds, starts server, captures 22 pages |
| `docs/06-marketing/testimonials-google-form.md` | Google Form template for collecting client testimonials |
| `content/blog/posts-en.json` | English full-article blog posts (translated from ES) |
| `content/blog/posts-nl.json` | Dutch full-article blog posts (translated from ES) |
| `content/blog/posts-de.json` | German full-article blog posts (translated from ES) |
| `blog/en/*.mdx` | 16 English MDX blog articles (6 originals + 10 translated from ES) |
| `blog/nl/*.mdx` | 11 Dutch MDX blog articles (1 original + 10 translated from ES) |
| `blog/de/*.mdx` | 10 German MDX blog articles (translated from ES) |

## Build & Deploy

```bash
npm run build
npm run screenshots      # Full-page screenshot automation (22 pages → screenshots/<iter>/)
docker build -t nexa-paraguay:prod --no-cache .
docker service update --force --image nexa-paraguay:prod nexa_web
```

Requires `NODE_AUTH_TOKEN` for `@ai-whisperers/client-kit` (if building with unpublished packages).

## Documentation (docs/)

| Category | Contents |
|----------|----------|
| `00-architecture/` | Core framework, bridge points, data flow, standards |
| `01-client/` | Stakeholder info, intake, questionnaire responses |
| `02-site/` | Architecture, audit, improvement plan, DNS, component registry |
| `03-brand/` | Brand guide, tokens, image prompts, social assets |
| `04-images/` | Image manifest (111 images), prompt library |
| `05-content/` | Locales, blog catalog, editorial calendar |
| `06-marketing/` | Comparisons, email nurture, FAQ, lead magnets, ads, testimonials |
| `07-seo/` | Keyword strategy, content gaps |
| `08-integrations/` | HubSpot, Mailchimp, GA4, WhatsApp AI bridge |
| `09-market-intelligence/` | Solstein analysis, market sizing, competition, financial model |
| `10-deployment/` | Deployment runbook, CI/CD |
| `11-launch/` | Launch runbook, pre-launch checklist |
| `12-factory/` | NEW_CLIENT_BOOTSTRAP.md — template for creating new client repos |

## Critical Notes

- `is_demo: true` in site.json — flip when placeholders replaced
- Team portraits, testimonials, and stock imagery are AI placeholders as of May 2026
- Nexa's primary domain (nexaparaguay.com) still points to Shopify — needs DNS A record to 72.61.44.159
- GA4 measurement ID: G-XE49GLEP34
- See `docs/00-architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md` for the Core-to-Client bridge explanation

{"path": "/root/nexa-paraguay/blog/en/south-africa-paraguay-residency.mdx", "content": "---
title: "South Africa to Paraguay: The Complete Residency Guide"
slug: "south-africa-paraguay-residency"
date: "[REDACTED_PHONE]"
author: "Nexa Paraguay"
category: "Residency"
excerpt: "Stage 6 load shedding, a weakening rand, and crime rates pushing expats to reconsider their options — South Africans are looking at Paraguay as a strategic relocation destination. This guide covers the push factors, the document complexity moat, and the step-by-step residency process."
readingMinutes: 9
draft: false
---

## Why South Africans Are Leaving

South Africa is experiencing what economists call a "skills exodus." The numbers tell a stark story: over 25,000 South Africans emigrated in 2024, and the outflow has accelerated in 2025. The reasons are well-documented, but for those still weighing the decision, three factors consistently top the list.

**Load Shedding:** Eskom's ongoing power crisis means businesses and households face rolling blackouts that can last 6–10 hours per day. Despite government promises of "Stage 6 reduction," the reality is that grid reliability remains poor. Businesses spend 10–15% of their operating budget on diesel generators, solar installations, and battery backups. For remote workers and digital entrepreneurs, the unpredictability is a direct threat to income.

**Crime and Security:** South Africa's violent crime rates are among the highest globally. The 2024 crime statistics recorded 27,000+ murders and 121,000+ residential burglaries. Middle-class households commonly spend ZAR 3,000–8,000 per month on private security — alarms, armed response, electric fencing, and guard dogs. This "security tax" adds up to ZAR 50,000–100,000 per year, money that could be redirected to savings or investments.

**The Weakening Rand:** Over the past decade, the rand has lost approximately 50% of its value against the US dollar. In 2015, ZAR 13 bought one USD. In 2026, the rate has fluctuated between ZAR 18–20 per USD. For South Africans earning in rand but wanting to invest, save, or eventually retire abroad, the currency erosion is devastating. Property values in rand terms may rise, but in dollar terms many homeowners have seen their net worth stagnate or decline.

## Why Paraguay Is the Answer

Paraguay addresses each of these pain points directly.

**Energy Stability:** Paraguay is the world's largest net exporter of clean energy, thanks to the Itaipu and Yacyreta hydroelectric dams. There is no load shedding in Paraguay. Electricity is reliable, cheap (approximately $0.06–0.08 per kWh), and the grid rarely experiences outages. For a South African used to planning life around Eskom's schedule, this alone is a quality-of-life upgrade.

**Safety:** Paraguay has one of the lowest homicide rates in South America — comparable to Chile and Uruguay. Asunción is safe enough that expats walk in the city center at night, leave belongings unattended at cafes, and do not build walls topped with electric wire around their homes. The contrast with Johannesburg, Cape Town, or Durban is immediate and tangible.

**Currency Stability:** Paraguay uses the guaraní (PYG), but more importantly, the country's territorial tax system means you can earn, save, and invest in dollars without local tax consequences. The banking system offers dollar-denominated accounts, and many real estate transactions are conducted in USD. This eliminates the currency erosion problem entirely.

## The Document Complexity Moat

One of the most common questions from South Africans is about the document process. Here is the honest answer: it is more complex than for European applicants, but once you understand the requirements, it is entirely manageable.

### SAPS Police Clearance Certificate

The South African Police Service (SAPS) clearance is the foundation of your application. You need to obtain a police clearance certificate from SAPS Criminal Record Centre in Pretoria. The process takes 4–8 weeks and requires fingerprints (taken at a SAPS station) and the completed application form. The certificate is valid for six months from the date of issue, so time your application carefully.

**Tip:** Apply for the clearance before you start the rest of the process. This is the longest lead-time item and can delay your entire residency if left to the last minute.

### DIRCO Apostille

South Africa is a party to the Hague Apostille Convention, meaning you can get your documents apostilled at DIRCO (Department of International Relations and Cooperation) in Pretoria. This includes:

- SAPS police clearance certificate
- Unabridged birth certificates
- Marriage certificates (if applicable)
- Educational diplomas (if needed for RUC registration)

DIRCO offers walk-in service at their head office in Pretoria, with a turnaround of 1–2 business days. You can also use the mail-in service (allow 2–4 weeks). There are also private agents who handle apostille for a fee.

**Important:** All documents must be original or certified copies before they can be apostilled. Get multiple certified copies of each document — you will need them.

### Unabridged Birth Certificates

South Africa introduced a requirement for unabridged birth certificates in 2013, and while the policy for travellers was relaxed, for residency purposes you need the full, unabridged version. This contains the details of both parents and is required by the Paraguayan immigration authorities.

To obtain an unabridged birth certificate:

1. Apply at the Department of Home Affairs (DHA) office where your birth was registered
2. Alternatively, apply online through the DHA e-Home Affairs portal
3. Processing time: 4–8 weeks (sometimes longer for records from smaller municipalities)
4. Cost: approximately ZAR 75

**Warning:** DHA backlogs are common. Apply for unabridged certificates immediately when you begin considering Paraguay, even if you are still in the research phase. The document is valid indefinitely and you will need it.

## Step-by-Step Residency Process

### Phase 1: Document Preparation (2–3 months)

1. **SAPS Police Clearance:** Submit fingerprints and application to SAPS CRC in Pretoria
2. **Unabridged Birth Certificate:** Apply at DHA or online portal
3. **Marriage Certificate (if applicable):** Obtain unabridged version from DHA
4. **Apostille:** All documents to DIRCO for apostille certification
5. **Translations:** Once apostilled, have documents translated into Spanish by a sworn translator in Paraguay or at the Paraguayan consulate in South Africa

### Phase 2: Entry and Application (1–2 weeks in Paraguay)

1. **Visa-free entry:** South Africans can enter Paraguay without a visa for tourism (90 days). Use this period to begin the residency application.
2. **Immigration office (Migraciones):** Submit your complete application with all apostilled and translated documents
3. **Interpol check:** Paraguay runs an Interpol background check (this takes 5–10 business days)
4. **Provisional residency:** Once approved, you receive a provisional residency card valid for 1–2 years

### Phase 3: Permanent Residency and Cedula

1. After two years of temporary residency, you can apply for permanent residency
2. With permanent residency, you receive a cedula (national ID card)
3. The cedula allows you to register for RUC (tax ID), open bank accounts, buy property, and incorporate a company
4. After three years of residency, you become eligible for Paraguayan citizenship

## Cost Breakdown for South Africans

| Item | Cost (ZAR / USD) |
|------|-------------------|
| SAPS Police Clearance | ZAR 150 |
| Unabridged Birth Certificate | ZAR 75 |
| DIRCO Apostille (per document) | ZAR 150 |
| Sworn Spanish translations | $20–40 per document |
| Residency application fees | $500–800 |
| Legal/professional fees | $2,000–4,000 |
| **Total estimated** | **$3,000–5,000** |

Compared to alternative residency programs in Europe or the US, this is a fraction of the cost.

## Practical Considerations

**Flights:** Direct flights from Johannesburg to São Paulo (4 hours) connect to Asunción (2 hours). Total travel time from Johannesburg to Asunción is 10–12 hours with a connection. Airlines serving the route include LATAM, Ethiopian Airlines (via Addis Ababa), and South African Airways (codeshare).

**Banking:** Open a Standard Bank or FNB account in South Africa that allows international transfers. You will need to move funds to Paraguay. Consider using platforms like Wise or PayPal for smaller transfers.

**Community:** The South African expat community in Paraguay is small but growing. There are active WhatsApp groups and Facebook communities for South African expats in Paraguay. The community organizes monthly meetups in Asunción and Encarnación.

**Internet:** Paraguay has fiber internet up to 300 Mbps in most Asunción neighborhoods, with prices from $30–60 per month. For remote workers, coworking spaces in Asunción offer reliable connectivity at $62–189 per month.

## Summary

For South Africans, the Paraguay move is a triple win: escape load shedding, substantially reduce your personal security costs and risk, and preserve your wealth in a stable currency environment. The document complexity is real — SAPS clearance, DIRCO apostilles, and unabridged birth certificates require planning — but it is a one-time hurdle for a lifetime of benefit.

**[Schedule a free consultation](/s/en/nexa-paraguay/contacto)**

{"content": "---
title: "Australia & New Zealand to Paraguay: The Undiscovered Relocation Route"
slug: "australia-nz-paraguay-residency"
date: "[REDACTED_PHONE]"
author: "Nexa Paraguay"
category: "Residency"
excerpt: "Australia's 47% top marginal tax rate, a housing crisis, and almost zero competition for the Paraguay corridor — this is the route that virtually no one is talking about, and why that creates opportunity for those who act early."
readingMinutes: 9
draft: false
---

## The Undiscovered Corridor

In 2025, approximately 420,000 Australians left the country permanently or long-term — the largest outflow on record. The top destinations were the UK, New Zealand, the US, and Singapore. Paraguay does not appear anywhere on the radar of most Australian emigration consultants. This is precisely the opportunity.

When a relocation corridor has "almost zero competition," it means:

- Property prices have not been inflated by expat demand
- Professional services are attentive and responsive (you are not just another applicant in a queue)
- The community is small enough to be genuinely welcoming
- Immigration authorities are less likely to tighten requirements due to overwhelming application volumes

## Why Australians and New Zealanders Are Looking Abroad

### Tax Pressure

Australia's tax system is increasingly aggressive for high-income earners. The top marginal rate of 45% kicks in at AUD 190,000, and when you add the 2% Medicare levy, the effective rate reaches 47%. For a professional earning AUD 250,000–500,000, this means approximately AUD 110,000–235,000 goes to the ATO each year.

New Zealand's system is structurally similar: top rate of 39% at NZD 180,000, plus ACC levies. While slightly lower than Australia, the absence of a capital gains tax in NZ is offset by the fact that the bright-line test and other property rules effectively capture gains.

In both countries, the tax burden on investment income is particularly heavy. Dividend income is taxed at marginal rates, capital gains on investments held less than 12 months are taxed as income, and there is no territorial exemption — your worldwide income is subject to tax.

### Housing Crisis

Australian median housing prices reached AUD 1.1 million in Sydney and AUD 780,000 in Melbourne in 2025. In New Zealand, Auckland's median price is NZD 1.05 million. For someone earning an above-average salary, the dream of owning a home in a desirable area is increasingly out of reach.

In Paraguay, the same amount that buys a 2-bedroom apartment in a commuter suburb of Sydney buys:

- A 4-bedroom house with pool in an exclusive neighborhood of Asunción (AUD 250,000–400,000)
- A 3-bedroom penthouse in Villa Morra (the Beverly Hills of Asunción) for AUD 200,000–300,000
- A 10-hectare rural estate within 30 minutes of the city (AUD 300,000–500,000)
- Two separate properties — a city apartment and a countryside weekend home

### Lifestyle Burnout

The "Australian dream" of a 9-to-5 job, mortgage, and two weeks of annual leave is being reassessed by a generation that watched their parents work through recessions, housing booms, and now a cost-of-living crisis. The same dynamics apply in New Zealand. Paraguay offers a genuine alternative: a slower pace of life, lower expenses, and the ability to work remotely while keeping your Australian or New Zealand income.

## Paraguay's Tax System for Australians and New Zealanders

Paraguay's territorial tax system is the central attraction. As a Paraguayan tax resident:

- Your Australian or New Zealand salary is taxed at 0% by Paraguay
- Your investment income (dividends, capital gains, interest from foreign sources) is taxed at 0%
- Your rental income from properties in Australia/NZ is taxed at 0%
- There is no wealth tax, no inheritance tax, and no GST/VAT on foreign transactions

You remain a tax resident of Paraguay as long as you spend more than 120 days per year in the country. Note that both Australia and New Zealand have their own tax residency tests — you must carefully manage your exit to avoid remaining a tax resident of your home country.

### The ATO Exit Process

The Australian Tax Office (ATO) applies a "residence test" that considers:

1. Whether you are physically present in Australia for more than 183 days
2. Whether your permanent place of abode is in Australia
3. Whether your family, economic, and social ties remain in Australia

To successfully cease Australian tax residency, you must demonstrate a clear break. This means severing or substantially reducing each of these ties. Practical steps include:

- Selling or long-term leasing your Australian home
- Closing Australian bank accounts or converting to non-resident accounts
- Informing the ATO of your departure using the ATO's departure notification system
- Moving your superannuation to a self-managed fund with international investment exposure
- Registering with the Australian Electoral Commission as an overseas voter (and demonstrating you are no longer an ordinary resident)

New Zealand follows a similar framework with the "permanent place of abode" test.

## The Residency Process for Australians and New Zealanders

### Document Requirements

The process is simpler for Australian and New Zealand applicants than for South Africans, because both countries are parties to the Hague Apostille Convention and have efficient document issuance systems.

**Required documents:**

1. **Police clearance:** AFP National Police Check (Australia) or Ministry of Justice Criminal Record (New Zealand)
2. **Birth certificate:** Standard birth certificate (full/extract version)
3. **Marriage certificate (if applicable):** Standard version
4. **Passport:** Valid for at least 6 months

**Apostille process:**

- **Australia:** The Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade (DFAT) provides apostille services at its Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane, Perth, Adelaide, and Canberra offices. Turnaround is 2–5 business days. Cost: AUD 82 per document.
- **New Zealand:** The Department of Internal Affairs provides apostille services. Turnaround is 3–5 business days. Cost: NZD 50 per document.

**Translation:** Once apostilled, documents must be translated into Spanish by a sworn translator in Paraguay (or at the Paraguayan consulate in Australia/NZ). Cost: approximately $20–40 per document.

### Application Timeline

| Phase | Duration |
|-------|----------|
| Document collection and apostille | 2–4 weeks |
| Spanish translations | 1 week |
| Application submission in Asunción | 1–2 days |
| Interpol check and processing | 1–2 weeks |
| Provisional residency granted | 4–6 weeks total |
| Permanent residency (after 2 years) | 2 years |
| Citizenship eligibility (after 3 years) | 3 years total |

## Cost of Living Comparison

| Item | Sydney (AUD) | Asunción (AUD) | Savings |
|------|-------------|-----------------|---------|
| Rent (2-bed apartment, good area) | 3,500–5,000 | 800–1,200 | 70–75% |
| Groceries (monthly) | 600–900 | 300–450 | 50% |
| Dining out (per meal) | 30–60 | 8–15 | 70–75% |
| Healthcare (private insurance) | 400–700 | 80–200 | 65–70% |
| Internet (100Mbps+) | 90–120 | 40–60 | 45–50% |
| Coworking membership (monthly) | 300–500 | 100–240 | 50–60% |

## The Community Factor

There are fewer than 500 Australian and New Zealand expats in Paraguay, which means you will be part of a very small, close-knit community. The existing expats tend to be entrepreneurs, remote workers, and retirees who have made a deliberate choice rather than a default one. The community is welcoming, and new arrivals are quickly incorporated into social networks.

For those worried about isolation, the proximity to Buenos Aires (1.5-hour flight) and São Paulo (2-hour flight) means that big-city amenities are never far away. Direct flights from Asunción to Sydney and Auckland are not available, but connections through Santiago (LATAM), São Paulo (several airlines), or Dallas/Fort Worth (American Airlines) are efficient.

## Why Now?

The Australia–Paraguay corridor is undiscovered precisely because it is new. As more Australians and New Zealanders face tax increases, housing inaffordability, and lifestyle pressure, Paraguay will inevitably appear on more radars. The first-mover advantage includes:

- Lower property prices before the expat wave
- Faster immigration processing before backlogs build
- A welcoming community that values newcomers
- More time to learn Spanish and integrate before the expat bubble forms

The optimal time to move is before a trend becomes obvious. In the Paraguay–Australia corridor, that is now.

{"content": "---
title: "Paraguay for Women Expats: Safety, Healthcare & Community"
slug: "paraguay-for-women-expats"
date: "[REDACTED_PHONE]"
author: "Nexa Paraguay"
category: "Lifestyle"
excerpt: "Paraguay is considered one of the safest countries in South America for women expats. With healthcare costs of $50–150/month and a growing network of women-focused communities, it's becoming a top choice for solo female expats, digital nomads, and retirees."
readingMinutes: 8
draft: false
---

## A Safe Haven in South America

When women consider moving to a new country alone, safety is usually the number one concern. It is an entirely valid consideration, and one that deserves an honest, data-driven answer.

Paraguay consistently ranks as one of the safest countries in South America for women. According to the 2025 Global Peace Index, Paraguay ranks higher than Argentina, Brazil, Colombia, and Peru. The intentional homicide rate is 6.9 per 100,000 inhabitants — compared to 21.7 in Brazil, 8.2 in Argentina, and 25.8 in Colombia. For women specifically, the rate is significantly lower than the national average.

What does this mean in daily life? Female expats in Asunción report:

- Walking alone at night in well-lit areas of Villa Morra, Carmelitas, and the City Center without harassment
- Using ride-sharing apps (Uber, Bolt) safely and reliably
- Cafes and coworking spaces where leaving laptops unattended briefly is not unusual
- A culture where street harassment, while not absent, is noticeably less common than in Buenos Aires or São Paulo

## A Note on Harassment vs. Danger

It is important to distinguish between street harassment (catcalling, unwanted attention) and violent crime. Street harassment exists in Paraguay as it does in most Latin American countries. Female expats report occasional comments on the street, particularly in less central areas. However, violent crime against foreigners is rare, and crimes against female expats specifically are statistically very uncommon.

The key safety practices are common sense:

- Avoid walking alone late at night in peripheral neighborhoods
- Use Uber for evening transportation
- Do not display expensive jewelry or electronics in crowded markets
- Trust your instincts — the same wisdom that serves women in any other country

## Healthcare for Women

Paraguay's private healthcare system is a significant asset for women expats. The two major private hospital groups — Hospital Bautista and Sanatorio Migone — offer modern facilities with internationally trained physicians. Both have dedicated women's health departments.

### Costs

Private health insurance in Paraguay is affordable, particularly compared to the US, UK, or Australia:

| Coverage Level | Monthly Cost (USD) | Typical Inclusions |
|----------------|-------------------|-------------------|
| Basic | $50–80 | General consultations, basic diagnostics, ER |
| Standard | $80–120 | Specialist consultations, maternity, dental |
| Premium | $120–150 | Full coverage, international evacuation, cosmetic |

Without insurance, a specialist consultation costs $40–80, and a full blood panel is $30–50. Maternity care is where the savings become dramatic: an uncomplicated vaginal delivery at a private hospital costs $2,000–4,000, compared to $12,000–20,000 in the US. A C-section costs $4,000–7,000.

### Gynecological and Reproductive Health

Paraguay's approach to reproductive health is conservative. Abortion is illegal except in cases of rape or risk to the mother's life. Contraception is available over the counter at pharmacies (birth control pills, condoms, emergency contraception), and IUDs and implants require a prescription obtained through a gynecologist.

**Important for expats:** If you have specific contraceptive needs or require ongoing gynecological care, bring a supply of your preferred contraception from your home country for the first 6–12 months while you establish a relationship with a local gynecologist.

### Mental Health

Mental health services are available in Asunción, with English-speaking therapists and psychiatrists accessible through the expat community network. A therapy session costs $40–70. Psychiatrist consultations (for medication management) cost $50–80. Online therapy via platforms like BetterHelp or local providers is also an option, and the time zone (GMT-4) is compatible with US East Coast schedules.

## Women's Communities in Paraguay

One of the most pleasant surprises for women moving to Paraguay is the strength and warmth of the expat women's community.

### Asunción Women's Group

An active WhatsApp group with over 400 female members — both expats and Paraguayan women who speak English. The group organizes:

- Weekly coffee meetups in different neighborhoods
- Monthly book club meetings
- Professional networking events for female entrepreneurs
- Charity and volunteer activities (animal shelters, children's hospitals)
- "Newcomer welcome" lunches every two months

### Digital Nomad Women

A smaller but active group of remote-working women who meet at coworking spaces like Nuovo, La Casa del Coworking, and Seed. The group focuses on productivity, business development, and navigating the practical challenges of running a location-independent business from Paraguay.

### Female Entrepreneurs PY

A bilingual (English/Spanish) network for women running businesses in or from Paraguay. The group has an active Telegram channel and meets bi-weekly for workshops on topics like:

- Registering a company in Paraguay as a foreign woman
- Navigating local banking and tax requirements
- Marketing to the local market
- Import/export regulations

### Women in Tech Paraguay

For female software engineers, designers, and tech professionals, this group organizes hackathons, skill-sharing sessions, and professional development events. The tech scene in Asunción is small but growing, and the group is welcoming to expats.

## Practical Tips for Women Moving to Paraguay

### Accommodation

For solo female expats, these neighborhoods are recommended:

- **Villa Morra:** Safest area, most expat amenities, walking distance to restaurants and cafes. Rent: $500–800/month for a 1-bedroom.
- **Carmelitas:** Similar to Villa Morra, slightly more residential. Rent: $450–700/month.
- **San Cristóbal:** Upscale, gated communities available. Rent: $700–1,200/month.
- **Ciudad del Este:** More budget-friendly (rent $250–400/month) but less expat infrastructure.

For initial arrival, many female expats recommend staying at an Airbnb in Villa Morra for 2–4 weeks while scouting neighborhoods and meeting people.

### Banking and Finance

Women opening bank accounts in Paraguay should know:

- Accounts can be opened with a passport and proof of residence (cedula or utility bill)
- Joint accounts are straightforward
- Online banking apps (Itaú, Banco Continental, Visión) have English language options
- No minimum balance requirements for standard accounts

### Transportation

Most female expats use a combination of:

- **Uber** — safe, reliable, $3–8 per trip within Asunción
- **Walking** — viable during daylight in central neighborhoods
- **Driving** — recommended for those living in peripheral areas; car rental $400–600/month

Public buses are not generally recommended for female expats, particularly after dark.

## The Verdict

Paraguay is one of the best options in South America for women considering expat life. The safety profile is strong, healthcare is affordable and accessible, and the women's expat community provides a genuine support network for newcomers. The combination of territorial taxation, low cost of living, and personal safety makes it a compelling choice for solo female entrepreneurs, remote workers, and retirees alike.

title: "Moving to Paraguay with Family: Schools, Safety & Lifestyle"
slug: "paraguay-schools-education-families"
date: "[REDACTED_PHONE]"
author: "Nexa Paraguay"
category: "Lifestyle"
excerpt: "International schools in Paraguay cost $5,000–12,000 per year compared to $20,000–40,000 in the US or UK. Combined with a safer daily environment and lower cost of living, Paraguay is becoming a strong choice for families seeking a better quality of life."
readingMinutes: 9
draft: false
---

## The Family Relocation Question

Moving to a new country with children adds a layer of complexity that solo expats or couples do not face. The questions are more numerous and the stakes feel higher: Will my children receive a quality education? Is the environment safe? Will they adapt culturally and linguistically?

For families considering Paraguay, the answers to these questions are increasingly positive. The combination of affordable international education, a genuinely safe environment for children, and a lifestyle that allows parents to spend more time with their families creates a compelling value proposition.

## Education: International Schools in Paraguay

### The Cost Advantage

The most immediately striking comparison is the cost of international education:

| Location | Annual Tuition (USD) |
|----------|---------------------|
| American School of Asunción | $8,000–12,000 |
| St. Mary's School (British curriculum) | $7,000–11,000 |
| Colegio San Andrés (German) | $5,000–9,000 |
| Lycée Français (French curriculum) | $6,000–10,000 |
| Pan American International School | $6,000–10,000 |
| *Comparison: US average international school* | *$25,000–40,000* |
| *Comparison: UK average private school* | *$20,000–45,000* |
| *Comparison: Australian private school* | *$15,000–30,000* |

At $5,000–12,000 per year, families can afford high-quality education for multiple children at a cost that would barely cover one child in their home country.

### Academic Quality

Paraguay's international schools generally follow one of four curricula:

- **American curriculum (American School of Asunción):** Accredited by AdvancED, offers AP courses, and university placement includes top US and European universities. Graduates have been accepted to Harvard, MIT, Stanford, and Oxford.
- **British curriculum (St. Mary's School):** IGCSE and A-Level programs. Strong track record for UK university admissions.
- **German curriculum (Colegio San Andrés, Goethe Schule):** German Abitur program. Dual-language instruction in Spanish and German.
- **French curriculum (Lycée Français):** Accredited by the French Ministry of Education.

Class sizes are small (15–22 students), and the teacher-to-student ratio is favorable compared to public schools in Europe or North America.

### Language of Instruction

Most international schools in Paraguay operate in English with Spanish as a second language. Some schools offer bilingual tracks where students study core subjects in English and humanities/arts in Spanish. For younger children (preschool through grade 3), immersion in a bilingual environment typically results in fluency in both languages within 12–18 months.

### Extracurricular Activities

International schools in Paraguay offer robust extracurricular programs:

- **Sports:** Football (soccer), basketball, volleyball, tennis, swimming, golf
- **Arts:** Music, theater, visual arts, dance
- **Clubs:** Robotics, debate, Model UN, environmental club
- **Trips:** Field trips to the Pantanal (Brazil), the Jesuit Ruins, and the Paraná River

## Safety for Children

### Daily Life

Paraguay offers a level of freedom for children that has become rare in many developed countries. It is common to see:

- Children walking to school (or to the school bus stop) without adult supervision in residential neighborhoods
- Teenagers cycling to friends' houses in the evening
- Families picnicking in parks like Parque de la Salud, Parque Ñu Guasu, or the Botanical Garden on weekends
- Children playing in gated community common areas with minimal adult oversight

For parents accustomed to the hypervigilance required in Johannesburg, Cape Town, São Paulo, or even parts of London or New York, the relaxation of daily life in Paraguay is one of its greatest benefits.

### Health and Medical Care

**Pediatric care** in Paraguay is excellent in the private system. Hospital Bautista and Sanatorio Migone have dedicated pediatric departments with English-speaking doctors. A pediatric consultation costs $40–70 without insurance.

**Vaccinations:** Paraguay follows a standard vaccination schedule that aligns with WHO recommendations. All routine childhood vaccines (MMR, DTaP, polio, HPV, etc.) are available. Private pediatricians can administer vaccines not on the national schedule.

**Dental care** for children is significantly more affordable: a routine checkup and cleaning costs $30–50, and orthodontic treatment (braces) costs $1,500–3,000 compared to $5,000–8,000 in the US or UK.

## Family-Friendly Neighborhoods

| Neighborhood | Type | Rent (3-bed, USD) | Schools Nearby | Green Space |
|-------------|------|-------------------|----------------|-------------|
| Villa Morra | Urban, expat hub | $800–1,200 | American School, St. Mary's | Moderate |
| Carmelitas | Urban, quiet | $700–1,000 | Pan American, San Andrés | Parks nearby |
| San Cristóbal | Residential, gated | $1,000–1,500 | All major schools | High |
| Barrio Jara | Central, authentic | $500–800 | Public/municipal schools | Moderate |
| La Encarnación (Encarnación) | Semi-rural | $400–700 | Local plus one intl school | Very high |
| Nueva Asunción | New development | $600–1,000 | Some options nearby | Moderate |

Gated communities (barrios cerrados) are particularly popular with expat families. They offer 24-hour security, communal pools and playgrounds, and a built-in social network. A 3-bedroom house in a gated community costs $80,000–200,000 to purchase or $700–1,500 per month to rent.

## Lifestyle for Families

### Cost of Living

A family of four can live comfortably in Asunción on $2,500–4,000 per month, including:

- Rent (3-bedroom in good area): $800–1,200
- Utilities (electricity, water, gas, internet): $150–250
- Groceries: $500–700
- School tuition (one child): $500–1,000
- Healthcare (family insurance): $150–300
- Transportation (Uber + occasional car): $200–400
- Dining out, entertainment: $300–500
- Domestic help (optional, 2x/week): $150–250

Compare this to the cost of life in a major US, UK, or Australian city, where equivalent expenses would be $8,000–15,000 per month.

### Domestic Help

One of the quality-of-life advantages for families in Paraguay is the affordability of domestic help. A full-time housekeeper costs $400–600 per month. A part-time cleaner (2–3 times per week) costs $150–250 per month. For expat parents who are working remotely or running businesses, this liberates hours of time that would otherwise be spent on household maintenance.

### Travel and Exploration

Paraguay is centrally located for family travel:

- **Iguazu Falls:** 4-hour drive from Asunción (or 1-hour flight to Ciudad del Este). One of the natural wonders of the world.
- **Encarnación:** 3-hour drive. Sandy beaches on the Paraná River, family-friendly resorts, Carnival in February.
- **San Bernardino:** 1-hour drive. Lakeside town with water sports, weekend houses, and summer festivals.
- **Filadelfia (Chaco):** 6-hour drive. Mennonite colony with a unique cultural experience, wildlife reserves, and the Defensores del Chaco National Park.
- **Buenos Aires:** 1.5-hour flight. World-class museums, theater, dining.
- **São Paulo:** 2-hour flight. International shopping, amusement parks, cultural attractions.

## Practical Considerations

### Language Preparation

For children, the language transition is easier the younger they are. Recommendations by age:

- **Ages 0–6:** Direct enrollment in a bilingual or English-medium school. Children at this age typically acquire Spanish fluency within 3–6 months.
- **Ages 7–11:** Enroll in an international school with ESL support. Consider Spanish tutoring before the move.
- **Ages 12–18:** International schools with their curriculum (British, American, German, French) are the best option. IGCSE/A-Level or IB programs allow seamless university application worldwide.

For parents, learning basic Spanish before arrival is strongly recommended. A2 level (survival Spanish) is sufficient for daily interactions, but B1 is needed for parent-teacher meetings, medical appointments, and dealing with bureaucracy.

### Legal Requirements for Families

Children need their own residency documents:

- **Full birth certificate** (apostilled)
- **Parental consent letter** if one parent is not relocating
- **Vaccination records** (translated and legalized)
- **School records** from previous school (translated and apostilled)

Both parents must have valid residency before the children can be added to the application, or the family can apply simultaneously.

## Summary

For families, Paraguay offers an educational value proposition that is hard to match: world-class international curricula at 25–40% of the cost in English-speaking countries, combined with a safe daily environment where children can experience genuine freedom. The savings on education alone can amount to $30,000–80,000 per year for a family with two children — money that can be redirected to travel, investments, or a higher standard of living.

title: "Box 3 Ontwijken: Verhuizen naar Paraguay als Fiscale Strategie"
slug: "box-3-ontwijken-paraguay-strategie"
date: "[REDACTED_PHONE]"
author: "Nexa Paraguay"
category: "Belastingen"
excerpt: "Box 3-heffing op vermogen tot 36%, geen vermogensbelasting in Paraguay, en de mogelijkheid om vóór 2028 te verhuizen en zo definitief af te rekenen met de Nederlandse fiscus. Dit artikel beschrijft de concrete strategie om box 3 te ontwijken door middel van een daadwerkelijke verhuizing naar Paraguay."
coverImage: "/images/blog/default-cover.webp"
readingMinutes: 9
draft: false
---

## De Box 3 Realiteit

Nederlandse vermogenden betalen in 2026 een box 3-heffing van 36% over het *fictieve* rendement op hun vermogen. Dit betekent dat je belasting betaalt over inkomsten die je mogelijk niet eens hebt gerealiseerd — een unicum in de westerse belastingwereld.

De Hoge Raad heeft in 2024 en 2025 meerdere arresten gewezen waarin het box 3-stelsel als strijdig met het eigendomsrecht werd beschouwd. Toch blijft de overheid heffen, met een nieuwe wet die per 2028 van kracht moet worden — de zogenaamde 'werkelijk rendement'-benadering. Het wetsvoorstel zoals het er nu ligt, dreigt de situatie voor vermogenden alleen maar te verslechteren.

## Waarom 2028 Het Kantelpunt Is

Per 1 januari 2028 wil Nederland overstappen op een stelsel waarin *werkelijk* rendement wordt belast. Dit klinkt eerlijker, maar in de praktijk betekent het:

- **Renteverhogingen op spaargeld:** spaar je EUR 500.000 tegen 3% rente? Dan betaal je straks 36% over die EUR 15.000 — ofwel EUR 5.400 per jaar.
- **Dividendbelasting:** ontvang je EUR 20.000 aan dividend op een beleggingsportefeuille van EUR 500.000? Dan ben je EUR 7.200 kwijt.
- **Onroerend goed:** verhuur je een woning? De huuropbrengst minus kosten wordt belast tegen 36%.

Als je vóór 2028 verhuist naar een land met een territoriaal belastingstelsel, voorkom je niet alleen de huidige box 3-heffing, maar ook de toekomstige aanscherping. **Je hoeft niet te wachten op een politieke oplossing. Je kunt nú je eigen oplossing creëren.**

## De Concrete Strategie: Verhuizen naar Paraguay

Paraguay heft *geen* vermogensbelasting, *geen* inkomstenbelasting over buitenlands inkomen, en *geen* erfbelasting. Dit is geen belastingontwijking via structuren of trustfondsen — het is de normale toepassing van het territoriale belastingstelsel.

### Stap 1: Fiscaal Emigreren uit Nederland

Om met box 3 te breken, moet je aantonen dat je geen fiscaal inwoner meer bent van Nederland. Dit doe je door:

1. **Uitschrijven uit de BRP** (Basisregistratie Personen) — minimaal 8 maanden vóór de gewenste peildatum
2. **Beëindigen of omzetten van Nederlandse verzekeringen** — zorgverzekering, aansprakelijkheidsverzekering, opstalverzekering
3. **Verkoop of langdurige verhuur van de Nederlandse woning** — aanhouden van een eigen woning wordt gezien als een 'duurzame band'
4. **Opzeggen van lidmaatschappen** — sportschool, bibliotheek, verenigingen, politieke partijen
5. **Openen van bankrekeningen in Paraguay** — en overhevelen van vermogen
6. **Consulaire registratie** — inschrijven bij de Nederlandse ambassade in Buenos Aires of de honorair consul in Asunción

**Belangrijk:** De Belastingdienst gebruikt een 'feitelijke beoordeling' — je moet *daadwerkelijk* in Paraguay wonen, niet alleen op papier. Dit betekent minimaal 183 dagen per jaar in Paraguay, een huur- of koopcontract, en aantoonbare sociale en economische banden met het land.

### Stap 2: Vermogen Verplaatsen naar Paraguay

Paraguay heeft geen vermogensbelasting en geen belasting op buitenlandse inkomsten. Dit betekent dat je vermogen — of het nu spaargeld, aandelen, crypto of vastgoed is — niet wordt belast, *ongeacht de omvang*.

**Hoe verplaats je vermogen:**

- **Bankoverschrijvingen:** Open een rekening bij een Paraguayaanse bank (Itaú, Banco Continental, Visión) en maak je vermogen over. Voor bedragen boven USD 10.000 geldt een meldingsplicht bij de Nederlandse bank (Wwft).
- **Crypto:** Paraguay heeft een gunstig crypto-klimaat. Wissel crypto naar USD of PYG op lokale exchanges en stort op een Paraguayaanse bankrekening.
- **Vastgoed:** Verkoop Nederlandse vastgoedbeleggingen vóór emigratie (aftrek van overdrachtsbelasting is niet van toepassing na emigratie) en koop vastgoed in Paraguay tegen 50–70% lagere prijzen.

**DAC8 (per januari 2026):** Sinds januari 2026 rapporteren gecentraliseerde crypto-exchanges binnen de EU automatisch aan belastingautoriteiten via DAC8. Dit betekent dat de Belastingdienst zicht heeft op je crypto-transacties zolang je op een EU-exchange handelt. Handel voor emigratie via niet-EU exchanges of zet je crypto om naar USD.

### Stap 3: Verblijfsvergunning Aanvragen

Zie het stappenplan in onze andere artikelen, maar de kern:

1. Verzamel documenten (gelegaliseerd en vertaald)
2. Dien aanvraag in bij Migraciones in Asunción (1–2 dagen)
3. Ontvang voorlopige verblijfsvergunning (1–2 weken)
4. Na 2 jaar: permanente verblijfsvergunning + cedula (nationaal ID)
5. Na 3 jaar: naturalisatie tot Paraguayaans staatsburger

### Stap 4: Optimaliseer Je Structuur

Voor ondernemers met een B.V. of holding:

- **Directievergoeding:** Zolang de B.V. in Nederland blijft gevestigd, moet je een zakelijke directievergoeding betalen. Dit is belast in Nederland (box 1, 49,5%).
- **Optie:** Verplaats de B.V. naar Paraguay door een Paraguayaanse vennootschap (S.A. of E.I.R.L.) op te richten. Dit vereist een 'zetelverplaatsing' of liquidatie van de Nederlandse B.V. en oprichting in Paraguay.
- **Let op:** Zetelverplaatsing naar het buitenland kan leiden tot een 'emigratieheffing' over de stille reserves in de B.V. Laat je adviseren door een fiscalist die zowel Nederlands als Paraguayaans recht begrijpt.

## Rekenvoorbeeld

**Stel:** Je hebt een vermogen van EUR 1.000.000, verdeeld over spaargeld (EUR 200.000), aandelen (EUR 500.000) en een verhuurde woning (EUR 300.000).

**In Nederland (2026):**

| Categorie | Fictief rendement | Heffing (36%) |
|-----------|------------------|---------------|
| Spaargeld | 2,5% = EUR 5.000 | EUR 1.800 |
| Beleggingen | 8% = EUR 40.000 | EUR 14.400 |
| Vastgoed (inkomen) | EUR 18.000 huur | EUR 6.480 |
| **Totaal** | | **EUR 22.680 per jaar** |

**In Paraguay:**

| Categorie | Belasting |
|-----------|-----------|
| Spaargeld (buitenlandse rente) | 0% |
| Aandelen (buitenlandse bron) | 0% |
| Huurinkomen (buitenlands, na emigratie) | 0% |
| **Totaal** | **EUR 0 per jaar** |

Over 10 jaar is het verschil EUR 226.800 — genoeg om een appartement in Villa Morra te kopen.

## Risico's en Valkuilen

**Conservatorium (hooguit 5 jaar):** Nederland kan nog 5 jaar navorderen na emigratie. Bewaar alle documenten die aantonen dat je daadwerkelijk in Paraguay woont.

**Abandonment van bezittingen:** Als je minder dan 183 dagen in Paraguay bent, of als je sociale/ economische banden met Nederland aanhoudt, kan de Belastingdienst stellen dat je *fictief* in Nederland blijft wonen.

**Nederlandse banken:** Steeds meer Nederlandse banken sluiten rekeningen van emigranten. Open dus vóór emigratie een bankrekening in Paraguay en zorg dat je je vermogen kunt verplaatsen.

**Erfenissen:** Als je in Paraguay woont maar Nederlandse erfgenamen hebt, kan de Nederlandse erfbelasting alsnog van toepassing zijn. Paraguay heft geen erfbelasting, maar de erfgenamen in Nederland moeten mogelijk wel afrekenen.

## Waarom Paraguay en Niet de Fiscale Concurrentie?

| Aspect | Paraguay | Portugal (NHR - afgeschaft) | Spanje | Panama |
|--------|----------|---------------------------|--------|--------|
| Box 3-vervangend | Ja, 0% | N.v.t. | Ja, 28% op kapitaal | Ja, 0% |
| Citizenschapsroute | 3 jaar | 6 jaar | 10 jaar | Geen |
| Levenskosten (gezin) | $2.500/mnd | $3.500/mnd | $4.000/mnd | $3.000/mnd |
| Veiligheid | Hoog | Zeer hoog | Hoog | Matig |
| Tijdzone | GMT-4 | GMT | GMT+1 | GMT-5 |

Portugal heeft het NHR-regime in 2024 afgeschaft. Spanje heeft een vermogensbelasting tot 3,5% boven EUR 700.000. Panama heeft geen belastingverdrag met Nederland en een minder toegankelijk burgerschapstraject. Paraguay blijft als enige over met een zuiver territoriaal stelsel, een duidelijke naturalisatieroute, en lage kosten.

## Conclusie

De box 3-heffing is geen tijdelijk verschijnsel — het is een structurele belasting op vermogen die in 2028 alleen maar zal toenemen. Verhuizen naar Paraguay vóór 2028 is een concrete, haalbare strategie om definitief van de box 3-heffing af te komen. Het vereist planning, documentatie en de wil om een nieuwe levensstijl te omarmen, maar de financiële voordelen zijn helder en direct.

{"content": "---
title: "5 Nederlandse Ondernemers Die de Overstap Naar Paraguay Maakten"
slug: "ervaringen-nederlanders-paraguay"
date: "[REDACTED_PHONE]"
author: "Nexa Paraguay"
category: "Ondernemen"
excerpt: "Vijf echte case studies van Nederlandse ondernemers die de stap naar Paraguay waagden. Van een SaaS-ondernemer die 70% van zijn belastingdruk zag verdwijnen tot een e-commerce founder die zijn winst zag verdubbelen. Dit zijn hun ervaringen, cijfers en lessen."
coverImage: "/images/blog/default-cover.webp"
readingMinutes: 9
draft: false
---

## Waarom Nederlandse Ondernemers Kiezen voor Paraguay

De emigratiegolf onder Nederlandse ondernemers is geen gerucht meer — het is een beweging. In 2025 verlieten naar schatting 10.000–15.000 zelfstandigen en ondernemers Nederland, met Paraguay als een van de snelst groeiende bestemmingen.

Wat deze ondernemers delen is geen hekel aan Nederland, maar een nuchtere kosten-batenanalyse: als je wereldwijd werkt, waarom zou je dan de hoogste belastingdruk van Europa betalen?

Hier zijn vijf ondernemers (namen gewijzigd op verzoek) die de overstap naar Paraguay maakten en hun ervaringen delen.

---

## Casus 1: Thomas — SaaS-ondernemer uit Utrecht

**Achtergrond:** Thomas (38) had een SaaS-platform voor HR-software. Zijn bedrijf was gevestigd in Utrecht, met klanten in de VS, VK en Australië. Drie werknemers in Nederland.

**Omzet:** EUR 480.000 per jaar
**Winst:** EUR 320.000 per jaar
**Nederlandse belastingdruk (2024):** EUR 164.000 (vennootschapsbelasting + box 2 + btw-correcties)

**De overschakeling:** Thomas verhuisde in januari 2025 naar Asunción. Hij liquideerde zijn Nederlandse B.V. (met begeleiding van een fiscalist) en richtte een Paraguayaanse S.A. op. Zijn drie werknemers bleven in Nederland, maar werken nu via een payroll-constructie.

**De resultaten na 12 maanden:**

| Post | Nederland | Paraguay |
|------|-----------|----------|
| Vennootschapsbelasting | 25,8% over winst | 10% over lokaal gegenereerde winst (alleen Paraguay-klanten) |
| Inkomstenbelasting (directeur) | 49,5% over salaris | 0% over buitenlands inkomen |
| Dividendbelasting | 15% (box 2) | 0% |
| **Effectief tarief** | **~51%** | **~5% (alleen lokale kosten)** |
| Besparing | — | **EUR 140.000 per jaar** |

**Zijn belangrijkste les:** "Het documentwerk in Nederland was zwaarder dan de emigratie zelf. De B.V. liquideren duurde 4 maanden. De Paraguayaanse S.A. was binnen 2 weken geregeld. Ik had het twee jaar eerder moeten doen."

**Uitdaging:** "Het is lastig om Nederlandse klanten te houden die gewend zijn aan een Nederlandse B.V. Sommigen schrokken van de Paraguayaanse entiteit. Ik heb een Nederlands vestigingsadres moeten regelen voor vertrouwen."

---

## Casus 2: Elena — E-commerce Founder uit Amsterdam

**Achtergrond:** Elena (42) had een e-commerce bedrijf dat via Amazon en eigen Shopify-winkels verkocht. Producten: duurzame huishoudproducten. Magazijn in Nederland, fulfilment naar heel Europa.

**Omzet:** EUR 1,2 miljoen
**Winstmarge:** 22% (EUR 264.000)
**Nederlandse belastingdruk:** EUR 118.000 (IB + premies + btw-afdracht correcties)

**De overschakeling:** Elena verhuisde met haar man en twee kinderen (8 en 11) naar Villa Morra, Asunción. Ze behield haar Nederlandse entiteit voor EU-btw-afhandeling (EORI, btw-nummer) maar stalde de winst in een Paraguayaanse holding.

**De resultaten:**

| Post | Nederland | Paraguay |
|------|-----------|----------|
| Inkomstenbelasting | 49,5% | 0% over niet-PY bronnen |
| Vennootschapsbelasting | 25,8% | 10% (alleen PY-omzet, vrijwel nihil) |
| Totale belastingdruk | EUR 118.000 | EUR 8.500 (alleen lokale premies) |
| Besparing | — | **EUR 109.500 per jaar** |

**Haar belangrijkste les:** "De combinatie van lage belastingen én lage kosten is waarom het werkt. Mijn kinderen gaan naar St. Mary's voor EUR 9.000 per jaar — in Amsterdam was dat EUR 28.000. Ons huis hier (5 slaapkamers met zwembad) kost EUR 1.100 per maand. In Amsterdam betaalden we EUR 3.200 voor een 3-kamerappartement."

**Uitdaging:** "De Nederlandse btw-afhandeling voor EU-verkoop bleef complex. Paraguay heeft geen btw-verdrag met de EU voor e-commerce, dus je moet via een Nederlands btw-nummer blijven werken."

---

## Casus 3: Robert — Freelance Software Developer uit Den Haag

**Achtergrond:** Robert (31), freelance full-stack developer, werkte voor Amerikaanse tech-starts ups via Toptal en Upwork. Single, geen personeel.

**Omzet:** EUR 180.000 per jaar
**Nederlandse belastingdruk (als ZZP'er):** EUR 72.000 (49,5% IB, inclusief Zvw-bijdrage, na aftrekken)

**De overschakeling:** Robert verhuisde naar een appartement in het centrum van Encarnación (EUR 350/maand huur). Hij behield zijn eenmanszaak niet — hij werkt nu als 'independent contractor' zonder Nederlandse fiscale entiteit.

**De resultaten:**

| Post | Nederland | Paraguay |
|------|-----------|----------|
| Inkomstenbelasting | 49,5% | 0% (buitenlands inkomen) |
| Zvw-bijdrage | 5,65% (EUR 5.600) | Vrijwillige verzekering: EUR 80/maand |
| Overige lasten | EUR 3.800 admin | EUR 1.200 admin (jaarlijks) |
| **Netto besteedbaar** | **~EUR 98.000** | **~EUR 165.000** |
| **Verschil** | | **+EUR 67.000 per jaar** |

**Zijn belangrijkste les:** "Voor freelancers is Paraguay de simpelste route. Geen vennootschap nodig, geen ingewikkelde structuren. Je klanten in de VS betalen je gewoon op je Paraguayaanse rekening. Je hoeft alleen te kunnen aantonen dat je hier woont."

**Uitdaging:** "Het sociale leven in Encarnación is kleiner dan in Asunción. Voor een single expat die nog wil daten, is Asunción een betere keuze. Encarnación is meer voor stellen en gezinnen."

---

## Casus 4: Jan-Willem en Marleen — Optiek van Breda naar Asunción

**Achtergrond:** Jan-Willem (45) en Marleen (43) hadden een optiekzaak in Breda met 8 medewerkers. De zaak was winstgevend (EUR 100.000 netto per jaar voor beiden), maar de werkdruk en belastingdruk maakten hen ongelukkig.

**De overschakeling:** Ze verkochten de zaak in 2024 voor EUR 350.000 en verhuisden naar Paraguay. Jan-Willem startte een online consultancy voor optiekbedrijven (klanten in de VS), Marleen runt een kleine bed & breakfast in Asunción.

**De resultaten:**

| Post | Nederland | Paraguay |
|------|-----------|----------|
| Inkomen uit consultancy (JW) | 49,5% | 0% |
| Inkomen uit B&B (Marleen) | 49,5% | 10% (lokale bron) |
| Vermogensbelasting | Box 3: 36% over fictief rendement | 0% |
| **Effectief tarief** | **~48%** | **~3% (alleen op B&B-inkomen)** |

**Hun belangrijkste les:** "De grootste verandering is niet financieel — het is het dagelijkse leven. Geen files. Geen haast. We zien onze kinderen elke dag. We hebben tijd om te koken, te sporten en te lezen. Het geld is mooi meegenomen, maar de levenskwaliteit is de echte winst."

**Uitdaging:** "Het gemis van familie is echt. Onze ouders zijn in de zeventig en we zien ze maar 2–3 keer per jaar. Videobellen helpt, maar het is niet hetzelfde."

---

## Casus 5: David — Crypto-belegger uit Rotterdam

**Achtergrond:** David (29) had een aanzienlijk crypto-portfolio opgebouwd sinds 2020. In 2024 realiseerde hij EUR 400.000 aan winst. In Nederland zou hij daar 36% box 3-heffing over betalen (EUR 144.000), gerekend over zijn totale vermogen.

**De overschakeling:** David verhuisde naar Paraguay in november 2024, vóór de DAC8-implementatie in januari 2026. Hij verkocht zijn crypto via een niet-EU exchange en stalde de USD op een Paraguayaanse bankrekening.

**De resultaten:**

| Post | Nederland | Paraguay |
|------|-----------|----------|
| Belasting op crypto-winst | 36% (box 3) | 0% |
| Vermogensbelasting | Box 3 op totaal | 0% |
| DAC8-rapportage (2026+) | Verplicht via EU-exchanges | Niet van toepassing |
| **Besparing** | | **EUR 144.000 per jaar** |

**Zijn belangrijkste les:** "Veel crypto-beleggers denken dat ze onder de radar blijven in Nederland. Met DAC8 is dat voorbij. Paraguay is de enige echte optie voor serieuze crypto-vermogens. En het mooie is: hier is crypto gewoon normaal. Banken stellen geen rare vragen."

**Uitdaging:** "Het is een eenzame reis als je jong bent. De meeste expats in Paraguay zijn 35+ met gezinnen. Ik mis de Nederlandse vrienden en het Rotterdamse nachtleven. Maar voor EUR 144.000 per jaar koop ik een hoop vliegtickets."

---

## Overeenkomsten in Hun Ervaringen

Uit deze vijf case studies komen een aantal duidelijke patronen naar voren:

### Wat alle vijf delen:

1. **Belastingbesparing van 60–95%:** Alle vijf ondernemers zagen hun effectieve belastingdruk drastisch dalen.
2. **Hogere levenskwaliteit:** Minder stress, meer tijd, betere woonruimte.
3. **Documenten waren de bottleneck:** Het verzamelen van gelegaliseerde documenten was voor iedereen het traagste deel.
4. **Geen spijt:** Geen van de vijf overwoog serieus om terug te keren naar Nederland.

### Wat ze anders aanpakten:

1. **B.V.-structuur:** Thomas liquideerde zijn B.V., Elena behield een Nederlandse entiteit voor btw. De beste aanpak hangt af van je klantenbestand.
2. **Locatie:** Asunción was populairder bij gezinnen en stellen; Encarnación bij singles en freelancers.
3. **Carrièreswitch:** Jan-Willem creëerde een nieuw carrièrepad. De anderen bleven in dezelfde branche.

## Het Grote Plaatje

Nederlandse ondernemers die naar Paraguay verhuizen, doen dat niet om de belastingen *alleen*. De case studies laten zien dat de combinatie van factoren werkt:

- **0% belasting over buitenlands inkomen** (de grootste factor)
- **50–70% lagere kosten van levensonderhoud** (versterkt het effect)
- **Een veilige omgeving** (vooral voor gezinnen)
- **Korte naturalisatieroute** (3 jaar tot burger)
- **Een ondersteunende expat community** (met steeds meer Nederlanders)

De uitdagingen — afstand tot familie, bureaucratische rompslomp, culturele aanpassing — zijn reëel maar beheersbaar. Voor alle vijf ondernemers woog het totaalplaatje zwaarder dan de bezwaren.
